retrieval.query_summary: Retrieve rules for SMC and SnD confluence, risk management, and trading style constraints.

factors.name: HTF structure aligned
factors.notes: D1 and H12 are bearish, overall trend is bearish

factors.name: Valid Structural Entry Support
factors.notes: M1 bearish supply zone and order block present

evidence.content_preview: SnD: QML + SR/RS Flip + Fakeout patterns

raw_response.explainable_reasoning: The Crash 100 Index is in a dominant HTF bearish trend (D1 and H12 are bearish). Price has pulled back into a D1 bearish FVG (100664.964 to 100936.482), representing a high-probability Pro-Trend Reversal zone. On the lower timeframes, structure has shifted bearish with M30, M15, M5, and M1 all aligned bearish. A high-conviction SnD candidate has printed on the M1 timeframe: CRASH 100 INDEX_QML_KILLER_TYPE1_BEARISH_100719.805. This pattern is a verified QML+MPL+SR_FLIP+FAKEOUT+MARUBOZU setup with 11 confluences. The entry price is set at 100719.805, with a structural stop loss placed at 100731.245 (above the fakeout level and QML price). The take profit targets are constructed structurally: TP1 at 100694.902 (M1 swing low), TP2 at 100685.485 (the candidate's target), and TP3 at 100642.255 (M1 major swing low), achieving a highly favorable 6.77R ratio. This setup meets all mandatory confluence requirements and is graded as an A setup.

htf_bias.notes: D1 and H12 are bearish. Price is reacting off a D1 bearish FVG.

mtf_bias.notes: M30 and M15 are bearish, aligning with the HTF bearish trend.

evidence.content_preview: SL must be structural — placed beyond the invalidation level of the setup

stop_loss.reason: Placed above the fakeout level and QML price to ensure structural invalidation.
